{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query mentions that ZnPO4 setting time is 4-7 minutes. The document [20] discusses zinc oxide eugenol (ZnPO4) impression material and states that the setting reaction is initiated by the hydrolysis of ZnO to form zinc hydroxide, which requires water. Adding a drop of distilled water would actually speed up the setting reaction, not prolong it. Prolonging spatulation time, small increments of addition of powder to liquid, and mixing on a cooled glass slab are all methods that can be used to prolong the setting time of the material.", "answer_choice": "A"}
